description: Load the crystal structure of the doxorubicin-DNA complex (PDB: 1D12). Identify the 3-letter residue name of the intercalating drug. Find an intercalation site on Chain A (where the drug physically inserts between two DNA base pairs). Measure the structural distortion (backbone stretching) caused by the drug by calculating the distance between the C1' (often labeled C1*) atoms of the two adjacent nucleotides on Chain A that directly flank the intercalator. Create a publication-quality figure highlighting the intercalation site and save it as a PNG to /home/ga/PyMOL_Data/images/dna_intercalation.png. Write a report to /home/ga/PyMOL_Data/intercalation_report.txt containing exactly: 1) The PDB ID (1D12), 2) The 3-letter ligand code for the drug, 3) The residue numbers of the two adjacent DNA bases you measured on Chain A, 4) The measured C1'-C1' distance in Angstroms.
